query: I'm starting a new NeoHaskell project hosted on GitHub and want every pull request to be automatically reviewed without paying for CodeRabbit or SonarCloud. How do I set up a GitHub Actions workflow that computes the diff between the PR's base and head SHAs, pipes it through neohaskell-code-review, and posts the findings as a PR comment? I also need to know which secrets to configure under Settings.

We're migrating our Cart service from vanilla Haskell to NeoHaskell and our CI is GitLab. Can you generate the gitlab-ci.yml job that runs automated code review on every merge request, posts the output as an MR comment, and fails the pipeline when any blocker findings appear? Include the ANTHROPIC_API_KEY secret setup and any required project access token permissions.

how do i wire neohaskell code review into ci so it runs on every pr headlessly? we have a .github directory and dont want any third party saas. need the workflow yaml, the secret name for anthropic, and the pull-requests: write permission wired up correctly

Set up automated PR review for our Stock management NeoHaskell project on Azure DevOps. We already have azure-pipelines.yml. I need ANTHROPIC_API_KEY stored as a secret pipeline variable, the review posted as a pull request thread comment, and the stage gated so it only runs on PR builds not on branch pushes.

Our team just finished implementing the Library catalog feature in NeoHaskell. Before we merge feature branches I want CI to automatically catch any violations — things like using String instead of Text, or IO instead of Task — without me having to manually trigger a review. Can you scaffold the one-time CI pipeline setup for GitHub Actions that does this headlessly on every PR open and synchronize event?

neohaskell review ci bitbucket setup - we're on bitbucket pipelines and want pull requests reviewed automatically by claude code. need it to post a summary comment to the PR and exit non-zero on blockers. also what repository variable do i add for the anthropic key and what scope does the access token need?

I want to add a one-time CI configuration to our NeoHaskell Counter service repo so that every PR gets a severity-ranked review comment automatically, with FAIL_ON_BLOCKERS=true so the check goes red if anything critical slips through. We're on GitHub Actions. What's the complete workflow YAML including the fetch-depth: 0 checkout and the gh pr comment step?

Our NeoHaskell Counter repo is on GitLab and we want to stop manually running code review before every merge. Wire up an automated MR review job — full history checkout so the diff is accurate, run neohaskell-code-review via claude -p, post the result as an MR note, and document exactly which CI/CD variables we need to set (masked, protected) for both the Anthropic key and the GitLab token.

Can you run neohaskell-code-review on the diff between main and my feature/cart-checkout branch right now? I want a severity-ranked list of findings — blockers, majors, minors, nits — with file and line citations before I open the PR so I can fix things first.

Review my NeoHaskell pull request changes in src/Counter/Commands.hs and src/Counter/Events.hs. Specifically check whether I'm using Task correctly instead of IO everywhere, whether the trap table patterns are exhaustive, and whether any Text/String boundary issues crept in through the FFI layer.

Set up a GitHub Actions workflow for our NeoHaskell Stock service that runs neo build and neo test on every PR and push to main. Include Nix store caching via cachix and make the test step post a summary of failed specs as a check annotation. This is for build and test CI, not code review.

I want to write unit tests for the Cart checkout module in our NeoHaskell project using the project's testing conventions, then create a GitHub Actions workflow that runs those tests automatically on every push. Walk me through both the test file structure and the CI YAML.

Our NeoHaskell Library catalog PR adds a large event sourcing layer. Can you do a thorough code review of the changes — focus on domain modeling correctness, whether event names follow past-tense conventions, whether the entity update functions handle all variant cases, and whether any side-effecting code leaked outside Task boundaries?

We're setting up end-to-end feature tests for our NeoHaskell Stock service and want them to run in CI on every PR. Help me write the Hurl request files covering the happy path and error cases, then create the GitHub Actions workflow that spins up the service and executes the Hurl suite against it.

Add a GitHub Actions workflow to our NeoHaskell Counter repo that runs neo run in the background, waits for the HTTP health endpoint to return 200, then executes the integration test suite and tears down the process. The goal is automated smoke-testing of the running service on every PR, not static code review.

I want to automate code review for our TypeScript and Node.js monorepo on GitHub Actions — have Claude review every PR diff for common anti-patterns and post findings as a PR comment. What workflow YAML do I need, what secrets, and how do I make the check fail on critical findings?
